name: Simulate Ground-Based Observation from Space Telescope Data
description: Astronomers frequently need to degrade high-resolution space telescope images to simulate ground-based observations. Using the archival Hubble FITS image at ~/AstroImages/simulations/hst_eagle_halpha.fits, measure the maximum pixel intensity of the original image. Simulate atmospheric seeing by applying a Gaussian Blur with a Sigma of 12.0 pixels and simulate a coarser ground-based detector by scaling the image down to 20% of its original size (X and Y scale of 0.2). Measure the maximum pixel intensity of the degraded image. Save the degraded image as a FITS file to ~/AstroImages/simulations/output/ground_eagle_halpha.fits and create a plain text report at ~/AstroImages/simulations/output/simulation_report.txt containing the following labeled lines: Original_Max, Simulated_Max, Final_Width, and Final_Height.
